mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and triggering negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 compared to single predictors.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during established expansion phases reflects strengthening manufacturing output, which precedes positive equity market momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: Short-term forward returns are negative in 60% of acceleration episodes, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day returns falls below 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annualized growth rate crosses above 2.5%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in 70% of threshold crossings, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < -0.02) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 indicates a healthy labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, generating positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 60% of high-payroll months, or the signal fails to achieve a statistically significant positive Rank IC (p > 0.05) across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales demonstrate synchronized strength in residential and consumer demand sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in 58% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising federal funds rates creates a stagflationary supply shock environment, compressing profit margins and forcing negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 65% of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to produce a negative Rank IC greater than -0.03 in walk-forward testing.
